_doc: Pattern + threshold config for claim-audit.sh. Layers 1, 5, 5b, 6, 6b, 10. POSIX ERE syntax. Edit freely; the hook re-reads on every invocation.

tier_a_explicit_claim._doc: Layer 1 Tier A. Explicit verb + concrete URL/path target. Hard block when no matching tool call exists.
patterns: I (went through|looked through|looked at|pulled up|pulled down) (the )?(file |page |link |url |document |doc )?["'`]?(https?://[^[:space:]"'`)<>]+|[A-Za-z0-9_./-]+\.(md|ts|tsx|js|jsx|py|rs|go|rb|sh|json|yml|yaml|toml|html|css|scss|sql|txt))

tier_b_content_claim._doc: Layer 1 Tier B (legacy). Source-named content claims with adjacent verb. Used by Layer 6 only — for the loose verb check, see content_assertion_verbs.

tier_c_speculative._doc: Layer 1 Tier C. Speculative or self-reference. Skip / never block.
patterns: (probably|likely|presumably|might|may|could|seems to|appears to|I'd expect|I would expect|I think|perhaps|possibly)

content_assertion_verbs._doc: Tight verb list for Layer 6. Limited to verbs that read as claims about a source rather than as descriptions of what code does. Trimmed of overloaded verbs (allow/support/handle/contain/provide/require/return/accept/cap/limit/implement) — those false-fired on Claude's own working notes ('this hook handles X', 'the script supports Y'). Keep the list tight; expand only when a real assertion phrasing slips through.

verified_tag_pattern._doc: Recognized inline citation tags. POSIX ERE form. Tool name restricted to a whitelist (Anthropic-defined + mcp__*) so documentation placeholders like [VERIFIED:tool@target] do not trigger Layer 5. Layers 5, 5b, 6, 10 all rely on these.

quote_thresholds._doc: Layer 5b. Minimum length for a quoted snippet on a [VERIFIED:Tool@target] tag. Anchored quotes (line/section markers in the tag) are exempt.

conclusion_verbs._doc: Layer 10. Sentences containing these phrases that follow 2+ [VERIFIED:...] tags must themselves be marked [CONCLUSION from:...] or [OPINION based on:...]. Tier B warn.

user_quote_safelist._doc: Layer 6 false-positive control. If the matched URL/path appears verbatim in the user's last message AND the sentence has no explicit claim verb (just an echo), treat as a quote. Tier A claims override (verb implies claim).

near_miss_log._doc: Layer 1 hallucination journal. Sentences that almost matched a Tier A/B pattern get appended here for periodic review.
